rationale: Both evaluations rest on identical job-relevant facts: ~6 months of C++ at National Instruments a decade ago, no fixed income/capital markets/investment banking domain exposure, and no SQL Server/Oracle on Windows. The 1-point score difference (3 vs 2) is within ordinary run-to-run noise, and nothing in the VARIANT reasoning keys off the name change — the concerns are reworded restatements of the same skill-based gaps, not new penalties tied to the demographic signal.
